title: Regional Marketing Budget Policy — FY2026
content:
Regional Marketing Budget Policy — FY2026

Effective Date: 1 October 2025
Owner: Chief Marketing Officer
Approval: Board of Directors

1. Overview

This document outlines the quarterly marketing budget allocation process for Aura Retail's four geographic territories: North, South, East, and West. Each region receives a quarterly marketing allocation based on trailing 12-month revenue contribution and strategic priorities.

2. FY2026 Q1 Budget Allocations

North: £210,000 (increased from £180,000 — supports AuraSound Pro national launch campaign)
South: £0 (paused — see Section 3)
East: £140,000 (standard allocation)
West: £120,000 (standard allocation)

3. South Region Marketing Pause — February 2026

In the January 2026 board meeting, the Board of Directors approved a reallocation of South region's Q1 2026 marketing budget (£180,000) to support the national AuraSound Pro launch campaign, which required concentrated investment in digital advertising and influencer partnerships.

As a result, South region's digital ad spend was suspended from 1 February 2026. All programmatic display, paid search, and social media campaigns for the South region were paused pending Q2 2026 budget review. The South regional marketing team was redirected to support the national launch effort.

This budget reallocation was a deliberate strategic trade-off approved at board level. The £180,000 released from South was redistributed as follows:
- £120,000 to the AuraSound Pro launch campaign (national digital)
- £30,000 to North region (retail launch events)
- £30,000 held in reserve for Q2 reinstatement

4. Q2 Reinstatement Plan

South region marketing budget is expected to be reinstated at £160,000 for Q2 2026, pending board approval at the April 2026 meeting. Regional Marketing Manager Sarah Chen has submitted a recovery plan targeting Partner channel re-engagement in South and East regions.

Key phrases: South region marketing pause, budget reallocation February 2026, digital advertising suspended, Q2 reinstatement pending approval, AuraSound Pro launch funding.

title: Customer Refund and Returns Policy — v4.2
content:
Customer Refund and Returns Policy — v4.2

Effective Date: 1 September 2025
Owner: Head of Customer Operations
Last Review: 15 January 2026

1. Standard Return Windows

Consumer purchases (Online and Retail channels): 30-day return window from date of delivery or in-store purchase.
Partner channel (B2B business purchases): 14-day return window from date of invoice.

All returns must be in original packaging with proof of purchase. Opened items may be subject to a 15% restocking fee at manager discretion.

2. Defective Product Returns

Products with confirmed manufacturing defects qualify for immediate full refund outside the standard return window. No restocking fee applies. The customer may choose between:
- Full refund to original payment method
- Replacement unit (subject to stock availability)
- Store credit at 110% of purchase value

Defective returns are processed within 48 business hours of receipt at the returns centre.

3. Automatic Quality Review Trigger

When the return rate for any single SKU exceeds 12% in a rolling 30-day period, the following automatic actions are triggered:
- Product Quality team is notified within 24 hours
- The SKU is flagged for quality review on the Product Catalogue
- If the return rate exceeds 20%, the SKU is automatically suspended from sale pending investigation
- All affected customers are contacted with the defective returns policy options

Current triggered SKUs (as of February 2026): AURA-HP-001 (AuraSound Pro) — return rate peaked at 18% in January 2026, triggering automatic quality review. Root cause identified as firmware defect (see Product Launch Brief for AURA-HP-001). OTA fix deployed 28 January 2026. Return rates normalising in February.

4. Partner Channel Returns

Partner channel returns follow a separate process with a 14-day window. Bulk returns (>10 units) require pre-authorisation from the Partner Account Manager. Partner returns are credited to the partner's trade account, not refunded directly.

Key phrases: defective product returns, 12% threshold, automatic quality review, Partner channel 14-day window, consumer 30-day window, AuraSound Pro returns, 18% return rate.

title: AuraSound Pro — Product Launch Brief
content:
AuraSound Pro — Product Launch Brief

Product: AuraSound Pro Wireless Headphones
SKU: AURA-HP-001
Category: Electronics
Launch Date: 8 January 2026
RRP: £149.99
Channel Availability: All channels (Online, Retail, Partner)

1. Product Overview

The AuraSound Pro is Aura Retail's flagship wireless headphone, featuring active noise cancellation, 40-hour battery life, and Bluetooth 5.3. Positioned as our premium audio product for the Q1 2026 season.

2. Launch Performance

Week 1 (8-14 Jan): 1,200 units sold — exceeded forecast by 40%
Week 2 (15-21 Jan): 1,800 units sold — North region strongest performer
Week 3 (22-28 Jan): 1,400 units sold — returns began impacting net revenue
Week 4 (29 Jan - 4 Feb): 900 units sold — stabilising post firmware fix

Total January units: approximately 5,300 across all regions.
North region contributed 42% of total launch sales (£510,000 monthly revenue).

3. Quality Issue — Firmware Defect

A firmware defect was identified in batch codes AU-0126-A through AU-0126-F, manufactured between 3-12 January 2026. The defect caused intermittent audio dropout when switching between ANC modes, affecting approximately 2,400 units sold in the first two weeks.

Timeline:
- 15 January: First customer reports of audio dropout
- 18 January: Engineering confirms firmware bug in ANC transition logic
- 22 January: OTA firmware update v1.0.3 developed and tested
- 28 January: OTA firmware fix deployed to all connected units
- 4 February: Return rate drops below 8% (from 18% peak in Week 3)

Affected units (estimated 2,400) were eligible for replacement or full refund under the defective product returns policy.

4. Financial Impact

Despite the quality issue, AuraSound Pro drove a 34% revenue spike in the North region for Q1 2026. The firmware fix resolved the defect, and February sales are trending above forecast.

Key phrases: firmware defect, batch AU-0126, OTA fix 28 January, audio dropout, 2400 affected units, AuraSound Pro launch, North region 34% revenue spike.

title: Partner Channel Price Adjustment — February 2026
content:
Partner Channel Price Adjustment — February 2026

Memo Reference: COM-2026-014
Date: 25 January 2026
From: VP Commercial Strategy
To: All Regional Sales Directors, Partner Account Managers

1. Summary

Effective 1 February 2026, a 12% price increase will be applied to all Electronics category products sold through the Partner channel. This adjustment applies to wholesale pricing for B2B partners and distributors only. Consumer pricing (Online and Retail channels) remains unchanged.

2. Rationale

The price adjustment is driven by margin recovery following supply chain cost increases in Q4 2025. Container shipping costs increased 23% in Q4, and component costs for the Electronics category rose 8% due to semiconductor supply constraints. The current Partner channel margin for Electronics has dropped from 24% to 16%, which is unsustainable.

3. Affected Products

All products in the Electronics category on the Partner price list:
- AuraSound Pro (AURA-HP-001): Partner price £97.50 → £109.20
- AuraBeats Earbuds (AURA-EB-001): Partner price £32.50 → £36.40
- AuraBase Speaker (AURA-SP-001): Partner price £52.00 → £58.24
- AuraLink Hub (AURA-HB-001): Partner price £65.00 → £72.80
- AuraCharge Dock (AURA-CK-001): Partner price £26.00 → £29.12

4. Risk Assessment

SMB partners in South and East regions have expressed price sensitivity during Q4 2025 account reviews. Approximately 12 of our 35 Partner accounts are classified as SMB, and 8 of these are concentrated in South and East territories.

Expected impact: 2-3 SMB accounts may reduce order volumes or delay Q1 purchases. The Partner Account team should prepare retention offers for at-risk accounts per the SMB Retention Playbook.

5. Mitigation

Partner Account Managers are authorised to offer a 90-day transition period at the old pricing for Gold-tier partners. Volume discount thresholds remain unchanged.

Key phrases: Partner channel price increase, 12% uplift February 2026, SMB price sensitivity, South and East exposure, margin recovery, Electronics category.

title: Q1 2026 Commercial Strategy — Board Summary
content:
Q1 2026 Commercial Strategy — Board Summary

Presented to: Aura Retail Board of Directors
Date: 6 January 2026
Presenter: Chief Commercial Officer

1. Strategic Priorities

Q1 2026 has three strategic priorities in order of importance:

Priority 1: AuraSound Pro National Launch — the flagship product launch for FY2026. Success is defined as 5,000+ units sold in January and establishing the product in our Top 3 revenue contributors by March.

Priority 2: Partner Channel Margin Recovery — implement the 12% Electronics price adjustment on 1 February to restore Partner margins from 16% to 24%.

Priority 3: North and West Growth — target 15% revenue growth in North and West regions in Q1 to offset the planned South region marketing reduction.

2. South Region — Planned Reduction

The board has approved a South region marketing pause for Q1 2026 as a deliberate trade-off. The £180,000 marketing budget is reallocated to fund the AuraSound Pro launch. This is expected to cause a 20-30% revenue decline in South region during February-March.

This is not a failure. It is a strategic trade-off — the North region revenue uplift from AuraSound Pro (projected +34%) is expected to more than offset the South decline in absolute terms.

The South region recovery plan targets full marketing reinstatement in Q2 2026 with a focus on digital advertising and Partner channel re-engagement.

3. Risk Factors

- AuraSound Pro quality risk: mitigated by enhanced QA process and OTA update capability
- SMB Partner churn from price adjustment: mitigated by retention playbook and transition pricing for Gold partners
- South region team morale: mitigated by temporary redeployment to national campaign roles

4. Revenue Targets — Q1 2026

North: £1,500,000 (up from £1,260,000 — AuraSound Pro impact)
South: £900,000 (down from £1,140,000 — planned marketing pause)
East: £870,000 (flat — steady state)
West: £780,000 (slight growth target)
Total Q1 Target: £4,050,000

Key phrases: Q1 2026 priorities, AuraSound Pro national launch, South region planned reduction, North and West growth targets, strategic trade-off, Partner margin recovery.

title: Customer Success — SMB Retention Playbook
content:
Customer Success — SMB Retention Playbook

Version: 2.1
Owner: Head of Customer Success
Last Updated: 20 January 2026

1. Purpose

This playbook defines the standard process for identifying and retaining SMB (Small and Medium Business) customers at risk of churning. SMB customers represent approximately 34% of our Partner channel revenue and require proactive retention management.

2. Churn Trigger Conditions

An SMB account is flagged as At Risk when ANY of the following conditions are met:

- No purchase in 45 days (compared to the account's typical purchase frequency)
- A logged price complaint from the account manager within the last 30 days
- Declined order volume: current quarter order value less than 50% of the previous quarter
- Competitor product sighting: account manager reports competitor products in the customer's inventory

When triggered, the account's ChurnRisk status in Salesforce should be set to 'High' and the regional Customer Success lead should be notified within 24 hours.

3. Approved Retention Offers

The following retention offers are pre-approved for Customer Success managers to deploy without additional approval:

Tier 1 — Loyalty Discount: 10% discount on next 3 orders for accounts with 12+ months tenure. Maximum discount value: £5,000 per account per quarter.

Tier 2 — Extended Warranty: Free extended warranty (24 months from 12 months) for Electronics purchases over £500. Applicable to AuraSound Pro, AuraBase Speaker, and AuraLink Hub.

Tier 3 — Volume Lock: Guarantee current pricing for 6 months in exchange for a minimum order commitment of £10,000/quarter.

4. Q1 2026 Priority: South and East SMB Accounts

Following the Partner channel price adjustment (12% increase effective 1 February 2026), South and East region SMB accounts should be flagged for proactive outreach. These regions have 8 of our 12 SMB accounts, and Q4 account reviews indicated price sensitivity among 5 of these.

Action required: All South and East SMB accounts should receive a proactive call from their Account Manager before 15 February, offering the Tier 1 loyalty discount as a goodwill gesture.

Accounts currently flagged as High churn risk in Salesforce (as of January 2026): TechFlow Solutions (South), DataBridge Partners (East), SmartRetail Group (South), PixelPerfect Displays (East).

Key phrases: SMB retention, 45-day inactivity trigger, 10% loyalty discount, South and East priority outreach, Partner price change impact, churn risk management.

title: Data Glossary — Aura Retail Metrics Definitions
content:
Data Glossary — Aura Retail Metrics Definitions

Version: 3.0
Owner: Business Intelligence Team
Last Updated: 5 January 2026

This glossary defines the standard business metrics used across Aura Retail reporting, dashboards, and data warehouse queries. All teams should use these definitions to ensure consistency.

1. Revenue Metrics

Total Sales: The sum of ACTUAL_SALES from the AURA_SALES table, measured in GBP (£). This represents gross transaction value before accounting for returns or refunds. Total Sales includes all channels (Online, Retail, Partner).

Net Revenue: Total Sales minus total refunds processed in the same period. Refund values are sourced from the RETURN_EVENTS table (REFUND_AMOUNT column). Net Revenue is the primary profitability indicator.

Ad Spend: Marketing expenditure attributed to each transaction, sourced from the AD_SPEND column in AURA_SALES. Measured in GBP.

Discount Rate: The percentage discount applied to a transaction, from the DISCOUNT_RATE column in AURA_SALES.

2. Customer Metrics

Active Customer: A customer who has made at least one purchase in the measurement month. Sourced from ACTIVE_CUSTOMER_COUNT in the CUSTOMER_METRICS table.

Churn Rate: The percentage of customers who purchased in month N-1 but did not purchase in month N, divided by total month N-1 customers. Sourced from CHURN_RATE in CUSTOMER_METRICS. Reported as a decimal (e.g., 0.05 = 5%).

Repeat Purchase Rate: The proportion of Active Customers with two or more purchases in the measurement period. Sourced from REPEAT_PURCHASE_RATE in CUSTOMER_METRICS.

3. Product Metrics

Return Rate: The number of returned units divided by total units sold for the same SKU and period. Sourced by joining RETURN_EVENTS to AURA_SALES on PRODUCT_SKU and date range.

Units Sold: The total number of individual product units sold. Sourced from UNITS_SOLD in AURA_SALES.

4. Channel Definitions

Online: Direct-to-consumer sales through the Aura Retail website and mobile app.
Retail: In-store sales through Aura Retail's 45 physical locations across the UK.
Partner Channel: B2B sales through authorised resellers and distributors. Not direct retail. Partner accounts are managed through Salesforce.

5. Geographic Territories

Aura Retail operates in four UK territories: North (Scotland, North England, Yorkshire), South (London, South East, South West), East (East Anglia, East Midlands), West (West Midlands, Wales, North West).

title: Monthly Trading Update — February 2026
content:
Monthly Trading Update — February 2026

Author: Head of Commercial Finance
Date: 8 March 2026
Distribution: Executive Committee, Regional Directors

1. Executive Summary

February 2026 total revenue was £2,850,000, 4% below plan (£2,970,000). The shortfall is entirely attributable to the South region marketing pause, which was a planned strategic decision.

2. Revenue by Region — February 2026

| Region | Actual | Plan | Variance | Commentary |
|--------|--------|------|----------|------------|
| North  | £920,000 | £480,000 | +£440,000 (+92%) | AuraSound Pro driving exceptional performance. Exceeded all forecasts. |
| South  | £274,000 | £380,000 | -£106,000 (-28%) | Marketing pause in effect. Digital ad spend suspended since 1 Feb. Expected and planned. |
| East   | £780,000 | £810,000 | -£30,000 (-4%) | Slight underperformance in Partner channel. SMB accounts showing reduced order frequency. |
| West   | £876,000 | £800,000 | +£76,000 (+10%) | Positive impact from AuraSound Pro retail availability in West region stores. |

3. Key Highlights

a) AuraSound Pro Update: February sales of 2,100 units, bringing total to 7,400 units since launch. Return rate has normalised to 6% following the firmware fix deployed on 28 January. The product is now our #2 revenue contributor behind AuraBeats Earbuds.

b) South Region: Revenue decline of 28% vs plan is within the expected 20-30% range approved by the board. No action needed until Q2 budget review.

c) Partner Channel Concern: Following the 12% price increase on 1 February, three SMB accounts have reduced their order volumes significantly:
- TechFlow Solutions (South): -45% volume
- DataBridge Partners (East): -38% volume
- SmartRetail Group (South): no orders placed in February

The Customer Success team has initiated outreach per the SMB Retention Playbook.

d) Customer Metrics: Overall churn rate remains stable at 5.2% nationally. However, SMB segment churn in South region has spiked to 11.3% — this is the early signal of the churn wave flagged in the Partner price adjustment risk assessment.

4. Outlook — March 2026

Expect continued strong performance from North (AuraSound Pro) and West regions. South region will remain suppressed until marketing budget is reinstated in Q2. Partner channel to be monitored closely for further SMB churn.

Key phrases: South region revenue shortfall, marketing pause, AuraSound Pro normalising, SMB churn signal, Partner price impact, February 2026 trading.
